items.title: Foreign rescue workers help with recovering Venezuela's quake victims
items.description: With little hope of finding loved ones alive, frustrated families of those affected by Venezuela's double earthquakes clamored for help to recover their loved one's bodies Friday as uncertainty grew over the death toll from the tragedy. Authorities on Friday reported a total of 2,645 deaths and more than 12,000 wounded from the 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes that struck the country last week, with most fatalities in the coastal town of La Guaira, where scores of residential complexes were flattened.

items.title: Pope Leo's visits to Lampedusa on July 4 'symbolic'
items.description: Pope Leo XIV, who has sparred with the Trump administration over its immigration crackdown, spent the Fourth of July on Saturday in the epicenter of Europe’s migration debate to honor the tens of thousands of people who have died trying to reach Europe to find freedom and prosperity. While the United States marks the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence with rallies, parties and fireworks, history’s first US-born pope traveled to the Sicilian island of Lampedusa to pray at a migrant cemetery and celebrate a solemn Mass for the island's newest arrivals. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports.

items.title: 'Not a proxy war': Sudan conflict 'would not go on like this' without international support
items.description: Pospisil says the siege has been ongoing for almost a year, with the RSF appearing determined to seize el-Obeid. While he argues that Sudan's civil war is not a proxy war in the strict sense, he also makes clear that "without international support, the war would not go on like this." Yet none of the external actors involved, whether the UAE, which supplies arms to the RSF, or the US and Saudi Arabia, has the leverage to bring the warring parties to a ceasefire, let alone a negotiated settlement.

items.title: Socceroos icon says Popovic 'messing with things too much' in GK switch
items.description: Mark Schwarzer says he is not a fan of Tony Popovic's decision to swap goalkeepers ahead of the penalty shootout, even if it had been successful.

items.title: Trump reads children's book on Usha Vance's podcast, then riffs on past presidents and himself
items.description: “I usually read stories about myself,” he said when asked if he has much time to read for fun while serving as President

items.title: Thirty-two years on, young Rwandans reflect on progress, pain and hope
items.description: For many young Rwandans, Liberation Day is as much about the future as the past.

items.title: Watch World Cup Day 23: Argentina and Egypt survive thrillers to advance
items.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

items.title: Artisanal potters in Benin are preserving their heritage
items.description: Women in Sè, a village in south-western Benin that is celebrated as the country’s pottery capital, carry on their family traditions.

items.title: Marcela Pagano: ‘It’s become evident that it is Karina Milei who calls the shots’
items.description: National deputy says Javier Milei’s government "has covered up Manuel Adorni’s corruption," that voters have fallen out of love with La Libertad Avanza and that Karina Milei, the President’s sister, is the one who’s really in charge in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Eneos secures enough crude through September, seeks source diversification

items.description: Argentines overwhelmingly disapprove of how President Milei handled corruption scandal involving his former Cabinet chief, according to a new poll. Leer más

items.title: Algeria out of World Cup after 2-0 loss to Switzerland
items.description: Switzerland is through to the World Cup Round of 16 after a 2-0 win over Algeria in Vancouver.

items.title: Tanzania deploys security forces to major cities ahead of planned protests
items.description: Demonstrators are expected to gather on 7 July to call for democratic reforms and justice for those killed in last year’s election violence.
